_meta.name: Epic Section Definitions
_meta.description: Section requirements for EPIC issue type. Epics are parent containers that coordinate multiple child issues toward a shared goal.

Summary.description: Clear description of the epic's overarching objective

Motivation.description: Why this epic matters - business value, user impact, or strategic initiative
quality_guidance: Explain why this matters to users or the project
Quantify impact where possible
Connect to strategic goals or product direction
Motivation.creation_template: [Why this epic matters - business value, user impact, strategic goal]

Integration Map.description: Systems and modules affected across all child issues
quality_guidance: Identify shared infrastructure touched by multiple children
Note cross-cutting concerns (auth, logging, config)
List shared test infrastructure
Use N/A for categories with no changes, don't leave blank
Integration Map.creation_template:
### Files to Modify
- TBD - requires codebase analysis

### Dependent Files (Callers/Importers)
- TBD - use grep to find references

### Tests
- TBD - identify shared test infrastructure

### Documentation
- TBD - docs that need updates

Impact.description: Priority, effort, and risk assessment across the full epic scope
quality_guidance: Include justification for priority (why P0? why P2?)
Estimate total effort across all children
Explain risk level considering aggregate change surface

Related Key Documentation.creation_template: _No documents linked. Run `/ll:normalize-issues` to discover and link relevant docs._

Session Log.description: Links to Claude Code JSONL session files involved in this epic's lifecycle

Goal.description: The overarching objective this epic delivers
Goal.question: What is the overarching objective this epic delivers?
quality_guidance: State the end-state clearly: what is true when this epic is done?
Connect to user or product benefit
Avoid listing child issues here — that belongs in Children

Scope.description: What is explicitly in scope and out of scope for this epic
Scope.question: What is in scope and what is explicitly out of scope?
quality_guidance: List what IS included to set expectations
List what is NOT included to prevent scope creep
If a child issue handles a specific slice, note it here

Children.description: Member issue IDs that make up this epic
Children.question: Which child issues are members of this epic?
quality_guidance: List each child as a bullet: - FEAT-NNN: short title
Group by phase or dependency order if helpful
Update as new children are captured or completed

Success Metrics.description: Measurable outcomes that indicate the epic is complete and successful
Success Metrics.question: How will you know this epic is successful?
quality_guidance: Prefer quantitative metrics (X% coverage, all N children done, 0 regressions)
Include acceptance tests or integration test scenarios
Consider both functional and non-functional criteria

full.description: Complete epic template with all applicable sections

minimal.description: Lightweight template with core sections only

EPIC: Goal should state the end-state clearly, not list child issues
Scope should have explicit in-scope and out-of-scope lists
Children should list all member issue IDs with short titles
Success Metrics should include quantitative criteria where possible
common: Impact must include justifications for priority, effort, and risk assessments
Integration Map should identify shared infrastructure across children
Integration Map should use 'N/A' for categories with no changes, not leave them as 'TBD'
